summary: Senior Full-Stack Engineer with 12+ years of experience building production systems and developer-facing platforms across Web3 security, B2B SaaS, marketplaces, and enterprise software. Recent OpenZeppelin work includes TypeScript-heavy product architecture, SDK capabilities for Web3 developers, EVM Relayer OSS ownership, KMS signer integrations, RPC failover, transaction cost handling, and a critical nonce race fix. Strongest fit for Zama is hands-on technical leadership where SDK ergonomics, partner integration friction, audit-aware blockchain tooling, and AI-assisted developer workflows all matter.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS work, including transaction cost handling, RPC failover, KMS signer integrations, and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.
Built SDK capabilities for Web3 developer integrations across Defender and OSS Relayer so technical teams could integrate products and build custom automation tooling.
Refactored the TypeScript monorepo and package architecture with ESM migration, package exports, bundler and tsconfig updates, reducing platform builds from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes.

Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building TypeScript and React workflows for smart contract audit delivery across findings, fix verification, client collaboration, and final PDF reports.

Supported the Defender sunset by closing feature gaps, improving documentation, and working directly with enterprise users during migration to OSS Relayer, which covered most enterprise Defender users.
Cut recoverable RPC and gas-spike alert noise by about 60% across Slack and PagerDuty, reducing wake-ups and making on-call incidents easier to triage.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a client-facing MyPremise feature with React and TypeScript that surfaced live data through one shareable URL instead of repeated PDF or PPT exports.
Contributed to the REST to GraphQL migration with Apollo across key entities, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.
Built an internal hackathon AI proof of concept using the OpenAI API for natural-language report generation.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Led an internal integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, saving $75,000 annually and enabling faster onboarding for customer service teams.
Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, migrating Python ETL logic so product and discount changes reached the app faster and contributed to a 21% CSAT increase.
Engineered a Redis-based conflict resolution layer for duplicate and out-of-order updates, improving product data accuracy and reliability by 40%.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Worked with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams to reduce release cycles from months to about two weeks.
Modernized key parts of the stack by moving from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, removing Internet Explorer-only constraints and enabling Chrome compatibility.
Introduced DevOps practices and led a staged migration to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients, working end to end from discovery to implementation across regulated and public-sector contexts.
Led architecture decisions for each project and set TypeScript, React, and React Native standards across the company.
Mentored five developers through pairing sessions, code reviews, and workshops focused on architecture, testing, and code quality.
Worked directly with clients through discovery calls, demos, and status updates.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Contributed to building Dxpress from idea to launch as one of the first Uber Eats-style delivery services in Chile.
Focused on Python and Go backend services for search, geolocation, real-time operations, and product ETL workflows.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on growing frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Zama is an open source cryptography company building Fully Homomorphic Encryption solutions for blockchain and confidential on-chain finance.
company_profile.mission: Make privacy practical by making end-to-end encrypted computation mainstream, including on public blockchains.
company_profile.vision: Confidential assets can be issued, traded, and managed natively on public blockchains with verifiability and programmable compliance.
company_profile.business_model: Open source protocol, SDK, and ecosystem infrastructure for confidential on-chain finance.
recent_signals: Zama positions the protocol as confidential on-chain finance for public blockchains using Fully Homomorphic Encryption.
The Zama SDK provides core TypeScript and React packages for shielding, unshielding, confidential transfers, signer adapters, and React hooks.
ERC-7984 offers encrypted balances, confidential transfers, public verifiability, and programmable compliance while preserving a familiar ERC-20-style developer workflow.
Zama highlights institutional deployments and ecosystem examples including T-REX, Bron, Raycash, Blockscout, and GSR.

source_notes.claim: Zama is an open source cryptography company building state-of-the-art Fully Homomorphic Encryption solutions for blockchain.
The Zama Protocol enables confidential assets to be issued, traded, and managed natively on public blockchains with full verifiability and programmable compliance.

The Zama SDK includes @zama-fhe/sdk and @zama-fhe/react-sdk for vanilla TypeScript, Node.js, React apps, signer adapters, confidential transfers, shielding, unshielding, React hooks, and cached decryption.
ERC-7984 provides encrypted balances, confidential transfers, public verifiability, and programmable compliance for financial institutions operating on-chain.
The role focuses on taking the Zama SDK from public beta to production-grade release for wallets, custodians, exchanges, and financial infrastructure.
